preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 48%, more than double England's 22% win probability. This represents a substantial gap: the model sees Argentina as the clear favorite, while England enters as a meaningful underdog in this neutral-venue encounter.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 30% under the model, suggesting competitive balance in play despite Argentina's overall edge. The spread between Argentina's win probability and England's is wide enough to indicate a tilted match, yet the draw frequency indicates neither team is overwhelmingly dominant—a tightly contested fixture is the statistical expectation.

The model and market diverge materially. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price England at roughly 35%, nearly 13 percentage points above the model's 22%. They see Argentina and the draw as closer (32% and 33% respectively), whereas the model leans heavily toward Argentina at 48%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; the divergence merits attention when forming an independent view.
